critic_findings.message: _atomic_update duplicates the mkstemp/rename/unlink-on-error pattern that already exists in sync_project_fields.py:105. The two implementations differ in one notable detail: sync_project_fields.py calls os.fsync() before os.rename; state_sync_ops.py does not. Neither file imports the other's helper.

monkeypatch.setattr(os, 'rename', boom) patches a stdlib call (os.rename) that is used internally by state_sync_ops._atomic_update. This is a borderline case: os is not code we wrote, but the patch reaches into the module under test's implementation detail rather than an external I/O boundary. Acceptable only if the test is explicitly testing the cleanup path — which it is — but the import of os inside the test function (line 182) should be moved to the top-level imports.
